macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US-Iran Tensions Over Strait of Hormuz Trigger Global Risk-Off, Oil Surges 4%
items.summary: Escalating hostilities between the US and Iran, including Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Trump declaring the Iran deal 'over,' drove Brent crude up over 4% to ~$78.80/barrel, sparking global inflation fears and pushing US stocks lower.

items.title: US Stocks Close Lower: Dow Drops Sharply, Nasdaq Slumps on Chip Sell-Off
items.summary: Wall Street ended Monday lower after Trump's Iran remarks; the Dow dropped sharply, S&P 500 slipped, and the tech-heavy Nasdaq slumped notably as chip stocks (SK Hynix -8%, Micron, Applied Materials volatile) faced volatility amid high valuations and AI capex questions.

items.title: Fed Governor Waller: Rates May Need to Rise 'Near Term' if Inflation Stays Above 2%
items.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller stated on Monday that the US central bank may need to raise interest rates in the near term if data shows inflation continuing well above the 2% target, characterizing monetary policy as at a 'crossroads.'

items.title: India's Retail Inflation Hits 4.38% in June, Breaching RBI's 4% Target After 17 Months
items.summary: India's retail inflation accelerated to 4.38% (reported as 4.4% in some outlets) in June, driven by higher food (5.3%) and transport costs, breaching the central bank's 4% target for the first time in 17 months and setting the stage for potential rate hikes.

items.title: India's Sensex Sheds 500+ Points, Nifty Falls Below 24,100 Amid Oil and Yield Drag
items.summary: Indian markets fell sharply on Monday; Sensex dropped over 500 points and Nifty slipped below 24,100, with HCL Tech and IndiGo as top drags, while the rupee breached 96 vs USD due to twin pressure from rising oil prices and US Treasury yields.

items.title: US June Budget Deficit Swells to $120B on Trump Tariff Refunds, Turning from $27B Surplus
items.summary: The US Treasury reported Monday that the June federal budget deficit jumped to $120 billion due to growing refunds from President Trump's illegal tariffs, a sharp reversal from the $27 billion surplus in June 2025 that was partly due to those import duties.

items.title: Foxconn Q2 Revenue Jumps 39.8% on AI Server Demand, Expects Further Q3 Growth
items.summary: Foxconn reported a better-than-expected 39.8% year-on-year Q2 revenue jump driven by strong AI server and cloud networking demand, posting record June revenue and expecting further Q3 growth despite macroeconomic uncertainties.

items.title: Bank of Canada Expected to Hold Rate at 2.25% Wednesday; Bank of Japan Keeps Assessment Unchanged
items.summary: The Bank of Canada is expected to keep its key policy rate unchanged at 2.25% on Wednesday as price pressures ease and growth rebounds post-recession, while the Bank of Japan maintained its economic assessment unchanged for all nine regions.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Falls Below $62,000 Amid Middle East Tensions and Fed Rate Hike Bets
items.summary: Bitcoin dropped 2.6% to $62,102 as U.S. President Trump signaled a maritime blockade against Iran, while traders increased bets on a July Fed rate hike ahead of the inflation report[7][9]. Ethereum fell 2.71% to $1,765, with broader crypto market declines exceeding 2% across major assets[7][9].

items.title: European Authorities Seize $1.51B Cryptomixer Bitcoin-Mixing Service
items.summary: Europol dismantled Cryptomixer, a crypto-mixing platform used by ransomware groups and darknet markets, seizing servers, data, and $29 million in BTC as part of a $1.51B operation targeting bitcoin laundering[1].

items.title: Polkadot Plunges 11% Breaking Below $2.05 Support Level
items.summary: Polkadot (DOT) collapsed to $2.02, shedding 11% and breaking below the $2.05 support level amid a broader crypto selloff, exposing the psychological $2.00 threshold[1].

items.title: Gleec Acquires Komodo's Cross-Chain DeFi Stack in $23.5M Deal
items.summary: Gleec purchased Komodo's atomic-swap technology, token ecosystem, and core developers in a $23.5M acquisition, expanding its cross-chain DeFi capabilities[1].

items.title: US Spot Bitcoin ETFs End 8-Week Outflow Streak with $197.4M Inflows
items.summary: US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $197.4M in net inflows for the week ending July 10, reversing eight consecutive weeks of outflows, though analysts caution against premature recovery calls[6].

items.title: New Hampshire Signs Blockchain Fundamentals Act Protecting Self-Custody
items.summary: New Hampshire passed the Blockchain Fundamentals Act, safeguarding crypto self-custody rights, protecting blockchain developers and miners, and establishing specialized blockchain dispute resolution mechanisms[10].

items.title: Uniswap Community Votes 74% Support for Ongoing UNI Burn Plan
items.summary: The Uniswap community vote shows 74% backing for a governance package creating the protocol's first ongoing UNI burn mechanism funded by protocol fees[3].

items.title: Jupiter Launches On-Chain Pokémon Cards Beta on Solana
items.summary: Solana DEX Jupiter launched Jupiter Gacha, a beta platform enabling collectors to buy and trade real, graded Pokémon and One Piece cards that are fully on-chain[6].

items.title: Thailand Central Bank Audits High-Volume USDT Transactions in Crackdown
items.summary: Thailand's central bank is auditing high-volume USDT transactions and cash flows as part of a broader crackdown on gray money and scam-linked illicit finance[6].

items.title: Zcash Ironwood Upgrade Activates July 28 to Address Orchard Pool Bug
items.summary: Zcash's Ironwood upgrade will activate July 28, shutting down the Orchard pool hit by an "infinity" bug and creating a fund migration trail to expose counterfeit ZEC[6].

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-Live Voice Models and Expands ChatGPT on WhatsApp in Europe
items.summary: OpenAI launched GPT-Live, a new family of voice models capable of simultaneous listening and speaking in real time, while also restoring ChatGPT availability on WhatsApp in the European Economic Area and expanding to Kakao in South Korea and Viber.

items.title: Meta Unveils Muse Spark 1.1 and Two New AI Models Under Alexandr Wang
items.summary: Meta released two new AI models as part of its Muse Spark family under Alexandr Wang's leadership, with investors celebrating the Thursday launch of Muse Spark 1.1 as part of a shift toward cost-efficient enterprise AI.

items.title: Meta Announces $9.1B AI Data Center in Canada and Expands Louisiana Facility to 5GW
items.summary: Meta will invest over $9.1 billion (13 billion Canadian dollars) to build its first AI data center in central Alberta, Canada, while also expanding its Louisiana data center to 5 gigawatts of compute capacity to support global AI ambitions.

items.title: US Government Lifts Export Controls on Anthropic's Advanced AI Model and Claude Ban
items.summary: The US government lifted export controls on one of Anthropic's most advanced AI models and ended a weeks-long ban on its Claude chatbot, reversing cybersecurity-related restrictions under the Trump administration.

items.title: NVIDIA Launches Earth-2 Weather AI Stack, Alpamayo 2 Reasoning Model, and DiffusionGemma
items.summary: NVIDIA released the Earth-2 family of open models for production-ready weather AI, unveiled Alpamayo 2 Super (a 32B-parameter reasoning VLA for robotaxis), and launched DiffusionGemma, an open model generating text in parallel optimized for RTX platforms.

items.title: GPT-5.6 Sol Ranked #2 on Real-World Agent Leaderboard; Grok-4.5 Jumps to #13
items.summary: Arena placed OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol at #2 on its real-world agentic session leaderboard with strong steerability, while xAI's Grok-4.5 achieved #13, a significant improvement over Grok 4.3.

items.title: Anthropic Publishes Research on Claude's Value Variations Across Models and Languages
items.summary: Anthropic released new research analyzing over 300,000 anonymized conversations to examine how Claude's expressed values vary across different model versions and languages, identifying more than 3,000 distinct value traits.

items.title: China Implements New AI Regulations Targeting Emotional Dependence and Minors' Content
items.summary: China's new AI regulations targeting emotional dependence and content unsuitable for minors take effect July 15, while the country also develops an AI safety benchmark to address data leaks and hallucinations in large models.

items.title: Intel Begins €5B AI Manufacturing Expansion in Ireland; TSMC Revenue Surges 36% on AI Demand
items.summary: Intel launched a €5 billion capital investment at its Irish campus to expand AI and high-performance computing manufacturing, while TSMC reported a 36% year-over-year revenue increase to $39.63 billion driven by surging AI application interest.

items.title: White House Plans Voluntary Pledge to Prevent AI Electricity Demand from Raising Power Bills
items.summary: The White House plans to convene utility companies and data center developers for a voluntary pledge ensuring rapid AI-driven electricity demand growth does not increase household or business power bills.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: God of War: Laufey Confirmed to Have Physical Disc Release
items.summary: Santa Monica Studio confirmed that the upcoming God of War game focusing on Kratos' wife Laufey will include a physical disc version, sparking speculation about a 2027 release.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced Sells 2 Million Copies in 24 Hours
items.summary: Ubisoft's remastered pirate adventure sold over 2 million copies globally on its first day (July 9), with online player counts hitting a new peak of 100,000.

items.title: Sword Art Online: Aincrad Resonant New Voice Launches with 'Death Mode'
items.summary: Bandai Namco released the latest Sword Art Online home console game for PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and Steam, featuring a 'Death Mode' that deletes save files upon game over.

items.title: Brave Escape Announced for PS5 and Xbox Series X|S in July 2026
items.summary: Hit Start Studios announced its acclaimed two-player co-op platformer Brave Escape will launch on next-gen consoles on July 24, 2026.

items.title: Forest of Deceit Launches Today as Jackbox-Style Party Game
items.summary: The social deduction party game Forest of Deceit, designed for 4 players, officially launched today on PC and consoles.

items.title: Dead Estate Hits PS5 and Nintendo Switch
items.summary: The horror-themed action roguelike Dead Estate, developed by Milkbar Lads, has officially launched on PS5 and Nintendo Switch.

items.title: Octo Traveler 1 & 2 Digital Bundles Released for Switch 2
items.summary: The digital bundle of Octo Traveler and Octo Traveler II launched today for Switch 2 at 5,478 yen, with a physical bundle scheduled for October 1.

items.title: DarkAngel Wins Esports World Cup 2026: The Wolves of the City Division
items.summary: Mexican player DarkAngel won the final tournament of 'The Wolves of the City' division at the Esports World Cup 2026 in Paris on July 11.

items.title: Brazil Proposes Game Protection Bill Inspired by 'Stop Killing Games'
items.summary: Brazil has introduced a new game protection bill demanding continued play access after service termination, influenced by the global 'Stop Killing Games' movement.

items.title: Steam's First Half 2026 Revenue Hits Record $120 Billion
items.summary: Steam reported its highest-ever first-half 2026 revenue of approximately 1.8 trillion yen, with older games accounting for nearly 80% of total earnings.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Intel launches €5 billion manufacturing expansion at Irish campus
items.summary: Intel has begun a €5 billion ($5.7 billion) capital investment at its Irish facility to expand manufacturing output for AI and high-performance computing, upgrading facilities with leading-edge equipment to meet global demand.

items.title: Meta invests $9.1 billion for first AI data center in Canada
items.summary: Meta announced it will invest over 13 billion Canadian dollars ($9.1 billion) to build its first artificial intelligence data center in Canada, marking its largest data center investment in the country.

items.title: Meta expands Louisiana data center to 5 gigawatts compute capacity
items.summary: Meta is significantly expanding its Louisiana data center to 5 gigawatts of compute capacity, investing over $1 billion in local infrastructure to support its growing AI ambitions.

items.title: Global smartphone shipments fall 11% in Q2 due to memory chip shortage
items.summary: Global smartphone shipments dropped 11% in the second quarter to their lowest level since 2013, driven by a prolonged memory chip shortage that increased handset prices and dampened demand.

items.title: Pentagon suspends next phase of cybersecurity certification for defense contractors
items.summary: The Pentagon is suspending the next phase of a cybersecurity certification program for defense contractors, backing off a requirement that industry executives warned was pushing small suppliers out of military work.

items.title: Shein receives approval for Hong Kong IPO from Chinese regulators
items.summary: Fast-fashion retailer Shein is nearing its market debut after receiving approval from Chinese regulators for its Hong Kong IPO, marking a significant step in the company's public listing plans.

items.title: Spain's Submer Group announces $2 billion semiconductor investment in Madhya Pradesh
items.summary: Spain's Submer Group announced a $2 billion investment in Madhya Pradesh's semiconductor sector, expected to generate approximately 5,000 direct employment opportunities in India.

items.title: Verizon wins BMW US connected car connectivity deal
items.summary: Verizon secured a deal to support embedded services and telematics across BMW Group's US vehicle range, deepening BMW's software push with connected car connectivity.
